data.title: Jacobs secures environmental services contract with US Air Force
data.description:
• Jacobs (NYSE:J) said on Wednesday that it has been selected by the U.S. Air Force Civil Engineer Center (AFCEC) to deliver architecture and engineering globally through a multiple award task order contract to support environmental restoration, environmental conservation, planning and environmental quality.
  • Under this contract, Jacobs will provide comprehensive environmental services to the U.S. Air Force, Air Force Material Command, Air Force Installation and Mission Support Centre. 
  • Support will include planning, investigation, assessment, design, construction phase design and field inspection.  
  • The U.S. Air Force and the AFCEC environmental program value the anticipated contract ceiling at $1.5 billion. Jacobs will provide services under a five-year base period and one five-year option period.

data.title: Citizens Financial declares $0.42 dividend
data.description:
• Citizens Financial (NYSE:CFG) declares $0.42/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.65%
  • Payable May 14; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.
  • See CFG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Lyft to acquire FREENOW for $197M
data.description:
• Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) said it will acquire European multi-mobility app FREENOW for $197M.
  • The firm will acquire the app from BMW and Mercedes-Benz Mobility.
  • FREENOW will continue operating as usual, and the deal transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2025.
  • "While there will be no immediate changes to FREENOW’s customer experience, over time, new benefits will be made available to FREENOW drivers and riders," the company said.

data.title: NexPoint Diversified REIT, NexPoint Hospitality Trust expect to close transaction on April 17
data.description:
NexPoint Diversified Real Estate Trust (NYSE:NXDT) and NexPoint Hospitality Trust (NHT.U:CA) anticipate that the acquisition of the latter will close on April 17.

NHT.U:CA's trust units are expected to be delisted from the TSX Venture Exchange at the close of trading on April 22.

data.description: *Other Operating Data Consensus Source: Bloomberg

data.title: Monster Beverage lands favorable legal ruling in old Bang Energy case
data.description:
Monster Beverage Corporation (NASDAQ:MNST) won a legal battle when a U.S. appeals court upheld its $311 million trial win in a case accusing former rival Bang Energy and its founder Jack Owoc of falsely advertising the ingredients and benefits of Bang's energy drinks. The company contended that it lost market share due to the false Bang claims.

The 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals rejected Owoc's legal challenges centered on the argument that a California federal court wrongly excluded evidence that would have helped their case. Monster Beverage's (NASDAQ:MNST) legal team noted that the decision recognizes that the trial court's rulings were plainly correct and that the jury's unanimous verdict in favor of Monster (MNST) is amply supported.

Monster Beverage (MNST) acquired Bang Energy and its parent company, Vital Pharmaceuticals, in July 2023 for approximately $362 million, following Vital Pharmaceuticals' filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in October 2022.

Shares of Monster Beverage (MNST) edged 0.2% higher in premarket trading on Wednesday. The beverage stock is up 11.6% on a year-to-date basis to defy the broad market decline.

data.title: Abbott in charts: Medical Devices sales up 10% Y/Y in Q1, down 3% sequentially
Abbott Laboratories Non-GAAP EPS of $1.09 beats by $0.02, revenue of $10.36B misses by $60M
data.description:
• Abbott Laboratories press release (NYSE:ABT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.09 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $10.36B (+3.6% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • First-quarter reported sales growth of 4.0 percent; organic sales growth of 6.9 percent or 8.3 percent when excluding COVID-19 testing-related sales

  • In the first quarter of 2025, total worldwide sales were $10.358 billion, total Diagnostics sales were $2.054 billion and COVID-19 testing-related sales were $84 million.

  • Abbott projects full-year 2025 organic sales growth to be in the range of 7.5% to 8.5%.

  • Abbott projects full-year 2025 adjusted operating margin to be 23.5% to 24.0% of sales.

  • Abbott projects full-year 2025 adjusted diluted earnings per share of $5.05 to $5.25 vs $5.15 consensus and second-quarter 2025 adjusted diluted earnings per share of $1.23 to $1.27.

data.title: IREN announces an increase in installed self-mining capacity
data.description:
IREN (NASDAQ:IREN) announced on Wednesday an increase in the bitcoin mining data centers operator's installed self-mining capacity to 40 EH/s from 37 EH/s.

The increase comes after the commissioning of additional mining hardware within the company's Childress Phase 4 (150MW) data centers.

IREN said it remains well-positioned to achieve 50 EH/s in H1 2025.

Shares were -1.47% pre-market to $5.37.

data.title: Perion Network expects Q1 sales above estimates
data.description:
• Perion Network (NASDAQ:PERI) expects revenue of approximately $88M for the first quarter of 2025, above analysts’ expectations of $83.69M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA to Contribution ex-TAC of approximately 4% for Q1 2025.
  • These preliminary results reflect a strong start to the year, supported by sustained momentum across the company’s growth engines and the early positive response to the launch of our Perion One strategy, the company said.
  • Perion will release its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 prior to the opening of the financial markets on May 13, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
